description: Piece 3 sync-mechanism registry scope. v2 flips the model: if a file is committable to git, it is in scope. Only gitignored-like paths and session-state files are excluded. See .planning/piece-3-labeling-mechanism/structural-fix/DECISIONS.md §Category 1 (D1.1–D1.8) for the canonical rule.

comments.rule: Include rule: committable = in scope. Excludes cover only gitignored-like paths + session-state + catalog data files (which are themselves records, not records-about-records).
comments.enumeration_source: scan.js enumerates via `git ls-files -co --exclude-standard` — what `git add .` would pick up. This honors .gitignore automatically, so any file that *is* gitignored is already out of scope at enumeration time (e.g. tools/statusline/jason-statusline-v*.exe). The excludes below layer on top for files that are committable but intentionally not cataloged (e.g. the catalog jsonls themselves).
comments.research_included: .research/** is fully IN scope as of v2 (D1.2). ~262 tracked files land in the catalog via the structural-fix re-run.
comments.tests_included: **/__tests__/** and **/*.{test,spec}.* are fully IN scope as of v2 (D1.3). Tests are first-class code; their type:test classification is a Piece 3 D3.1 + D4.6 addition.
comments.generated_included: Generated artifacts (17 .husky/_/ shims, package-lock.json, .research/research-index.jsonl, .planning/TODOS.md) are IN scope per D1.5 — stamped status:generated + portability:{not-portable | portable-with-deps} per D3.2.
comments.statusline_exe_excluded: tools/statusline/jason-statusline-v* is gitignored (machine-specific Go binary per CLAUDE.md §1). The explicit exclude here is belt-and-suspenders on top of the git-ls-files gitignore respect.
comments.scope_json_self: This scope.json file itself is in-scope via **/* (it is project-scoped per D8.5 — does not cross to SoNash via the mirror).
